speaker: BEDFORD
text: The day begins to break and night is fled,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Whose pitchy mantle over-veiled the Earth.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Here sound retreat and cease our hot pursuit.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Bring forth the body of old Salisbury,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And here advance it in the marketplace,

speaker: TALBOT
text: The middle center of this cursèd town.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now have I paid my vow unto his soul:

speaker: TALBOT
text: For every drop of blood was drawn from him

speaker: TALBOT
text: There hath at least five Frenchmen died tonight.

speaker: TALBOT
text: And, that hereafter ages may behold

speaker: TALBOT
text: What ruin happened in revenge of him,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Within their chiefest temple I’ll erect

speaker: TALBOT
text: A tomb wherein his corpse shall be interred,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Upon the which, that everyone may read,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Shall be engraved the sack of Orleance,

speaker: TALBOT
text: The treacherous manner of his mournful death,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And what a terror he had been to France.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But, lords, in all our bloody massacre,

speaker: TALBOT
text: I muse we met not with the Dauphin’s grace,

speaker: TALBOT
text: His new-come champion, virtuous Joan of Arc,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Nor any of his false confederates.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: ’Tis thought, Lord Talbot, when the fight began,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Roused on the sudden from their drowsy beds,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: They did amongst the troops of armèd men

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Leap o’er the walls for refuge in the field.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Myself, as far as I could well discern

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: For smoke and dusky vapors of the night,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Am sure I scared the Dauphin and his trull,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: When arm-in-arm they both came swiftly running,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Like to a pair of loving turtledoves

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: That could not live asunder day or night.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: After that things are set in order here,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: We’ll follow them with all the power we have.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: All hail, my lords. Which of this princely train

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Call you the warlike Talbot, for his acts

speaker: MESSENGER
text: So much applauded through the realm of France?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Here is the Talbot. Who would speak with him?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The virtuous lady, Countess of Auvergne,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: With modesty admiring thy renown,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: By me entreats, great lord, thou wouldst vouchsafe

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To visit her poor castle where she lies,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: That she may boast she hath beheld the man

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Whose glory fills the world with loud report.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Is it even so? Nay, then, I see our wars

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Will turn unto a peaceful comic sport,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: When ladies crave to be encountered with.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: You may not, my lord, despise her gentle suit.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Ne’er trust me, then; for when a world of men

speaker: TALBOT
text: Could not prevail with all their oratory,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Yet hath a woman’s kindness overruled.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: And therefore tell her I return great thanks,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And in submission will attend on her.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Will not your Honors bear me company?

speaker: BEDFORD
text: No, truly, ’tis more than manners will;

speaker: BEDFORD
text: And I have heard it said unbidden guests

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Are often welcomest when they are gone.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Well then, alone, since there’s no remedy,

speaker: TALBOT
text: I mean to prove this lady’s courtesy.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Come hither, captain.

speaker: TALBOT
text: You perceive my mind?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: I do, my lord, and mean accordingly.
